text: SubhanAllah walhamdulillah wa la ilaha illallah wallahu akbar
note: The four phrases most beloved to Allah. Say them, then type them.

text: Allahumma salli ala Muhammadin wa ala ali Muhammadin kama sallayta ala Ibrahima wa ala ali Ibrahim
note: Salawat — blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ. Reward for every letter.

text: The best of deeds are those done consistently even if they are small in number
Indeed with every hardship comes ease indeed with every hardship comes ease
note: Al-Inshirah 94:5-6 — Allah repeated it twice for emphasis.

text: Focus is not about saying yes to the thing you have to focus on it is about saying no to the hundred other good ideas
note: Deep work requires protecting your attention fiercely.

text: Hasbiyallahu la ilaha illa huwa alayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul arshil azim
note: Allah is sufficient for me. Recite 7 times morning and evening for protection.

text: Your body has a right over you your eyes have a right over you and your family has a right over you
note: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ — Bukhari. Balance is not laziness — it is sunnah.

text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest so never stop learning and seeking to improve
note: Seek knowledge from the cradle to the grave — a timeless principle.

text: And He is with you wherever you are and Allah is seeing what you do
note: Al-Hadid 57:4. You are never truly alone — Allah is always aware.

text: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billahil aliyyil azim
note: There is no power and no strength except with Allah. A treasure from Jannah.

text: Take care of your physical health because a healthy body carries a healthy mind and a focused heart
note: Your ability to worship and work depends on your physical state.

text: And whoever puts their trust in Allah then He will suffice them verily Allah will accomplish His purpose
note: At-Talaq 65:3. Tawakkul does not mean passivity — it means doing your part then trusting.

text: Eat less sleep enough exercise daily and you will think more clearly work more effectively and live longer
note: The Prophet ﷺ said: Fill one third with food one third with drink and one third with air.

text: Subhana Allahi wa bihamdihi subhana Allahil azim
note: Two phrases light on the tongue heavy in the scale beloved to the Most Merciful — Bukhari.

text: If you want to be productive do the most important thing first before your energy is drained by lesser tasks
note: Prioritize like the Prophet ﷺ who began the day with Fajr before all else.

text: Say my Lord increase me in knowledge for knowledge is the path to fearing Allah and drawing close to Him
note: Ta-Ha 20:114. The first word revealed to the Prophet ﷺ was Iqra — Read.

text: A good day begins with a good intention so before you open your laptop ask yourself why you are doing this work
note: Niyyah transforms ordinary actions into acts of worship.

text: The person who takes regular breaks accomplishes more by the end of the day than the person who powers through without rest
note: You are using QiyamBreak — you already understand this.
